LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER FOUNDATION AIDING THE ELDERLY'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF: PETITIONER FOUNDATION AIDING THE ELDERLY'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED. [SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS.] THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED AS MOOT. THIS MATTER IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 21, 2022. THE PARTIES ARE TO PREPARE A JOINT STATEMENT SETTING FORTH A PROPOSAL TO ENSURE SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE WITH HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SEC. 1420. IF THERE ARE AREAS OF DISAGREEMENT, THEN THE JOINT STATEMENT SHALL OUTLINE THOSE AREAS OF DISAGREEMENT. THE JOINT STATEMENT SHALL ALSO SET FORTH WHAT CPRA DISPUTES STILL EXIST. THE PARTIES SHALL FILE THE JOINT STATEMENT BY JANUARY 14, 2022. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Hearing Re: Petitioner Foundation Aiding The Elderly'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate/To Ensure Substantial Compliance With Health & Safety Code Sec. 1420
Off Calendar Jan-19-2022 Continued To Jan-31-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Motion For Writ Of Mandate Or, In The Alternative, For Declaratory Relief; **Court Reporter Fees Paid**
Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Joseph J. Mann, Esq. (415.913.7800; jmann@lexlawgroup.com) and Howard Hirsch, Esq. (hhirsch@lexlawgroup.com) of Lexington Law Group for Petitioner.
Jennifer C. Addams, Esq. (415.510.3363; Jennifer.Addams@doj.ca.gov) Deputy Attorney General for Respondents.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER FOUNDATION AIDING THE ELDERLY's Motion For Writ Of Mandate Or, In The Alternative, For Declaratory Relief:

Petitioner Foundation Aiding the Elderly's petition for writ of mandate is granted.

[see word minutes for details.]

The declaratory relief cause of action is denied as moot.

This matter is continued to January 21, 2022. The parties are to prepare a joint statement setting forth a proposal to ensure substantial compliance with Health & Safety Code sec. 1420. If there are areas of disagreement, then the joint statement shall outline those areas of disagreement. The joint statement shall also set forth what CPRA disputes still exist. The parties shall file the joint statement by January 14, 2022.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
